mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive trend in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) over the prior 3 months indicates expanding economic activity and rising corporate earnings, which pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of periods when PAYEMS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate crosses above 5.0%, monetary policy becomes restrictive enough to tighten financial conditions and pressure equity valuations downward over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical tightening cycles often preceded equity drawdowns
failure_condition: More than 60% of trigger instances produce positive 5-day forward returns, or the average return after crossing is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts month-over-month and a federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a housing-led economic slowdown that depresses corporate earnings and equity prices over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts peaked in early 2006 and rates were rising
failure_condition: In instances where HOUST declines and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, negative 60-day returns occur less than 50% of the time, or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle defined by a rising federal funds rate, an increase in industrial production stokes fears of further tightening and higher costs, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25bp over prior 3 months
failure_condition: When the trigger is active, rising INDPRO leads to positive 20-day returns more often than negative, or the mean return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) directly boosts consumer discretionary stocks and lifts overall market sentiment, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The Rank IC for RSAFS growth predicting 5-day returns is consistently below 0.03 over any trailing 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above the psychologically important 5% threshold signals an inflection toward labor market weakness, which historically precedes consumer spending retrenchment and equity market declines over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE crossed above 5% in past recessions
failure_condition: After crossing above 5%, the 60-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production posts strong year-over-year growth above 3% while the fed funds rate is already elevated above 4.5%, the economy appears to be overheating, raising the risk of accelerated tightening that depresses equity valuations over the short term.
evidence_refs: fact:2006 saw strong IP and rising rates
failure_condition: When the trigger condition holds, 5-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the mean return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth is decelerating, a decline in industrial production confirms that the slowdown is spreading to manufacturing, which historically leads to an equity market correction over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate declines (current < previous)
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth peaked in Q1 2006 and began decelerating
failure_condition: When GDP growth decelerates and INDPRO declines, negative 60-day returns occur less than 55% of the time, or the signal has no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02).
